---
suppress-bibliography: true
jupyter: python3
---

# Assessment {.unnumbered}

```{python}
#| label: set-ecosystem-code
#| echo: false

# Default ecosystem code for template development.
# This line is replaced by build_ecosystem_pages.py for each ecosystem.
ecosystem_code = 'M1.1.1'
```

```{python}
#| label: open-ecosystem-config
#| echo: false
import os
import yaml
from pathlib import Path

# Resolve config path relative to the project root.
project_root = os.environ.get('PIXI_PROJECT_ROOT', str(Path('..').resolve()))
config_path = Path(project_root) / 'config' / 'ecosystems' / ecosystem_code / 'ecosystem.yaml'

with open(config_path) as f:
    eco = yaml.safe_load(f)

# Extract commonly used fields.
name = eco.get('ecosystem_name', 'TODO')
index = eco.get('index')
authors = ', '.join(eco.get('authors', []))
credits = eco.get('assessment_credits', {})

# Page heading: prefix with the ecosystem index when available (the same
# numeric id encoded in the ecosystem COG).
heading = f"{index} · {name}" if index is not None else name
```

```{python}
#| label: load-ecosystem-data
#| echo: false
import sys
from rle.core import Ecosystems, criterion_b_status, rle_category

# Shared config helpers live in scripts/; make them importable from here.
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(project_root) / 'scripts'))
from _config import ensure_vector_source

country_config_path = Path(project_root) / 'config' / 'country_config.yaml'
with open(country_config_path) as f:
    country_config = yaml.safe_load(f)

source = country_config['ecosystem_source']
# ecosystem_code_column is optional: fall back to the name column.
ecosystem_column = source.get('ecosystem_code_column') or source.get('ecosystem_name_column')
# Prefer the ecosystem-sorted `optimized_data` copy when configured, so that
# filtering to one ecosystem uses parquet predicate pushdown instead of loading
# the whole national map into memory. Falls back to `data`.
ecosystems = Ecosystems.from_file(
    ensure_vector_source(source.get('optimized_data') or source['data']),
    ecosystem_column=ecosystem_column,
    ecosystem_name_column=source.get('ecosystem_name_column'),
    functional_group_column=source.get('functional_group_column')
)
ecosystem = ecosystems.filter(ecosystem_code)
if ecosystem.size() > 0:
    eoo = ecosystem.eoo
    aoo = ecosystem.aoo
else:
    eoo = None
    aoo = None
```

# `{python} heading` {.unnumbered}

<!-- Ecosystem index -->
<!-- [Ecosystem index:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} index if index is not None else 'N/A'` -->

<!-- Authors -->
[Authors:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} authors`

<!-- Biome -->
[Biome:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('biome', 'TODO')`

<!-- Functional Group -->
[Functional Group:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('functional_group', 'TODO')`

<!-- Global classification -->
[Global classification:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} ecosystem_code`

<!-- IUCN Status -->
[IUCN Status:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('iucn_status', 'TODO')`

<!-- Description -->
[Description:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('description', 'TODO')`

<!-- Distribution -->
[Distribution:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('distribution', 'TODO')`

<!-- Ecosystem map (COG) -->
[Ecosystem map:]{.parameter}&nbsp;

```{python}
#| label: display-ecosystem-cog
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
# Display the published ecosystem COG with a browser-only viewer. The COG is
# read client-side over HTTP range requests by assets/cog-viewer.mjs (deck.gl
# COGLayer via esm.sh), which selects the pyramid level for the current zoom
# (true level-of-detail) and reprojects ESRI:54034 on the GPU. THIS ecosystem's
# pixels are highlighted (crimson) over the muted other classes.
import json

raster_cfg = country_config.get('ecosystem_raster') or {}
cog_url = raster_cfg.get('cog_url')

if not cog_url:
    print(
        "::: {.callout-note}\n"
        "The ecosystem map raster (COG) has not been built yet. Run "
        "`python scripts/rasterize_ecosystem_to_cog.py` to generate and "
        "publish it, then re-render. This requires billing to be enabled on "
        "the GCP project — Cloud Storage buckets, unlike Earth Engine, cannot "
        "be created without it.\n"
        ":::"
    )
else:
    # Generated pages live at content/3_ecosystem_assessments/<code>/<code>.qmd,
    # so the shared viewer module (registered as a Quarto resource) is three
    # levels up. Deps are pulled from esm.sh inside the module.
    module_src = "../../../assets/cog-viewer.mjs"
    viewer_opts = {"url": cog_url, "index": index, "height": 520}
    print(
        f'<div id="ecosystem-cog-viewer" style="height:520px;width:100%;"></div>\n'
        f'<script type="module">\n'
        f'import mount from "{module_src}";\n'
        f'mount(document.getElementById("ecosystem-cog-viewer"), {json.dumps(viewer_opts)});\n'
        f'</script>'
    )
```

<!-- Characteristic Native Biota -->
[Characteristic Native Biota:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('characteristic_native_biota', 'TODO')`

<!-- Abiotic environment -->
[Abiotic environment:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('abiotic_environment', 'TODO')`

<!-- Key processes and interactions -->
[Key processes and interactions:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('key_processes_and_interactions', 'TODO')`

<!-- Major threats -->
[Major threats:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('major_threats', 'TODO')`

<!-- Ecosystem collapse definition -->
[Ecosystem collapse definition:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('ecosystem_collapse_definition', 'TODO')`

<!-- Assessment summary -->
[Assessment summary:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
**`{python} eco.get('assessment_summary', 'TODO')`**

<!-- Assessment information -->
[Assessment information:]{.parameter}&nbsp;

```{python}
#| label: build-criteria-table
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

def _status_cell(value):
    # Color-code a status cell by its RLE category (VU/EN/CR/LC/...); values that
    # are not a known category code (e.g. a placeholder) render plain.
    cat = rle_category(value)
    if not cat:
        return f'      <td>{value}</td>'
    bg = cat['background_color']
    fg = '#000' if bg in ('yellow', 'green', 'lightgray', 'white') else '#fff'
    return (
        f'      <td style="background:{bg};color:{fg};font-weight:bold;'
        f'text-align:center;">{value}</td>'
    )


def _build_criteria_table(criteria_status):
    rows = []
    criteria_order = [
        ("A", ["A1", "A2a", "A2b", "A3"]),
        ("B", ["B1", "B2", "subcriteria", "B3"]),
        ("C", ["C1", "C2a", "C2b", "C3"]),
        ("D", ["D1", "D2a", "D2b", "D3"]),
        ("E", ["E"]),
    ]
    for criterion, subcriteria in criteria_order:
        status = criteria_status.get(criterion, {})
        for i, sub in enumerate(subcriteria):
            value = status.get(sub, "NE")
            if i == 0:
                rows.append(
                    f'    <tr>\n'
                    f'      <td rowspan="{len(subcriteria)}">Criterion {criterion}</td>\n'
                    f'      <td>{sub}</td>\n'
                    f'{_status_cell(value)}\n'
                    f'    </tr>'
                )
            else:
                rows.append(
                    f'    <tr>\n'
                    f'      <td>{sub}</td>\n'
                    f'{_status_cell(value)}\n'
                    f'    </tr>'
                )
    return (
        '<table class="criteria-table">\n'
        '  <thead>\n'
        '    <tr>\n'
        '      <th colspan="2">Criteria</th>\n'
        '      <th>Status</th>\n'
        '    </tr>\n'
        '  </thead>\n'
        '  <tbody>\n'
        + "\n".join(rows) + "\n"
        '  </tbody>\n'
        '</table>'
    )

# Fill the Criterion B row (B1/B2) with the categories computed from the spatial
# metrics (EOO/AOO), overriding the manually-authored YAML values. B3 and the
# a-c sub-conditions stay as authored (not derivable from the spatial metrics).
criteria_status = dict(eco.get('criteria_status', {}) or {})
if eoo is not None or aoo is not None:
    _b = criterion_b_status(eoo_km2=eoo, aoo_cells=aoo)
    _b_row = dict(criteria_status.get('B', {}) or {})
    if _b['B1'] is not None:
        _b_row['B1'] = _b['B1']
    if _b['B2'] is not None:
        _b_row['B2'] = _b['B2']
    criteria_status['B'] = _b_row

print(_build_criteria_table(criteria_status))
print(
    '\n::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}\n'
    'Criterion B **B1 and B2 are computed from the EOO and AOO spatial thresholds** '
    '(IUCN RLE Guidelines v2.0). A final listing under B1/B2 also requires at least '
    'one of sub-conditions (a) continuing decline, (b) threatening processes, or '
    '(c) few threat-defined locations. Other criteria reflect the assessment config.\n'
    ':::'
)
```

<!-- Assessment outcome -->
[Assessment outcome:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
**`{python} eco.get('assessment_outcome', 'TODO')`**

<!-- Year published -->
[Year published:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('year_published', 'TODO')`

<!-- Date assessed -->
[Date assessed:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('date_assessed', 'TODO')`

<!-- Assessment credits -->
[Assessment credits:]{.parameter}&nbsp;

Assessed by: `{python} credits.get('assessed_by', 'TODO')`

Reviewed by: `{python} credits.get('reviewed_by', 'TODO')`

Contributions by: `{python} credits.get('contributions_by', 'TODO')`

<!-- Criterion A -->
[Criterion A:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('criterion_a_description', 'TODO')`

<!-- Criterion B -->
[Criterion B:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
AOO was measured as `{python} aoo if aoo is not None else 'N/A'` (10 km x 10 km) grid cells .
EOO was measured as `{python} f"{eoo:.0f}" if eoo is not None else 'N/A'` km².

<!-- Criterion C -->
[Criterion C:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('criterion_c_description', 'TODO')`

<!-- Criterion D -->
[Criterion D:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('criterion_d_description', 'TODO')`

<!-- Criterion E -->
[Criterion E:]{.parameter}&nbsp;
`{python} eco.get('criterion_e_description', 'TODO')`
